title: Gooey Molten Turkish Tofu Adana Kebap
description: A fatty and molten Turkish dish featuring silken tofu, cooked in a tagine style and served in the form of Adana kebap on a tava griddle, this shojin-friendly recipe is perfect for adventurous eaters. It combines the richness of Turkish cuisine with the simplicity of tofu.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a tava griddle over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Add the crumbled tofu, paprika, cumin, cayenne pepper, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Use a spatula to shape the mixture into long, thin strips resembling Adana kebap.
Cook the tofu mixture for about 10 minutes, flipping occasionally, until it is golden brown and crispy on the outside and molten on the inside.
Drizzle the lemon juice over the top of the kebap and serve immediately.
Garnish with fresh parsley or cilantro, if desired.
Serve with a side of steamed vegetables or crusty bread for a filling meal.
